> Phase 1 · Week 1 · Day 1 of LLM chapter  
> Orientation complete. Repo live. Time to learn.

### Today's Focus
- **Handbook chapter:** [LLM](https://aiweb3.school/en/handbook/llm.html) — Capabilities, limits, what models can't replace
- **WCB task:** Could not confirm from WCB page (client-rendered) — check manually at [WCB Learning](https://web3career.build/programs/AI-Web3-School#tab=learning)

### 🟡 Minimum Path (< 1 hour)
1. Skim the [LLM chapter](https://aiweb3.school/en/handbook/llm.html)
2. Add 3 bullets to this note: what you read, what was unclear, what you want to try
3. Draft and submit WCB check-in

### 🟢 Recommended Path (2 hours)
1. Read the full LLM chapter + take notes in this file
2. Run a quick experiment in `experiments/2026-05-20-llm-basics.md` or `.py` (e.g. compare model outputs for the same prompt with different system instructions)
3. File one item in `handbook-feedback/` if anything was unclear
4. Submit WCB check-in

### 🔵 Challenge Path (4 hours)
1. Read LLM chapter + annotate key limits (context window, hallucination, stochasticity)
2. Build experiment: prompt a model to summarize an on-chain transaction JSON — observe how it handles structured vs. unstructured data
3. Complete any open WCB task
4. Push everything to GitHub (`git add . && git commit -m "day 1: LLM chapter notes + experiment" && git push`)

## Orientation Session (completed earlier today)

---

## What I Did (Orientation)

- Bootstrapped the AI × Web3 School Learning Agent (Sensei via Claude/Cowork)
- Created personal learning repository structure in `AIxWeb3_School/`
- Confirmed learner profile: intermediate AI, familiar Web3, developer track, 2–4h/day
- Read the full Handbook overview: 4-layer structure (AI Foundations → Web3 Foundations → AI × Web3 Bridge → Frontier Exploration)
- Created AGENTS.md (agent rules) + CLAUDE.md symlink
- Created `learning-plan.md` with 3-phase plan across ~8 weeks

## Key Takeaways from Handbook Overview

- AI × Web3 is not just buzzwords — it requires simultaneous understanding of model context, tool orchestration, wallet permissions, on-chain execution, and verifiability
- Pure AI perspective underestimates asset/permission risks; pure Web3 perspective misses model context complexity
- My priority path: fill AI gaps first (LLM → Agent → MCP → Evaluation), then bridge topics, then frontier

## What I Want to Try Next

- Read the LLM chapter and write first experiment
- Check WCB learning page for today's assigned tasks
